name: A curated list of awesome projects related to eBPF.
description: A curated list of awesome projects related to eBPF.

links.title: ebpf.io
links.description: A gateway to discover all the basics of eBPF, including a listing of the main related projects and of community resources.

links.title: Cilium's BPF and XDP Reference Guide
links.description: In-depth documentation about most features and aspects of eBPF.

links.title: docs.ebpf.io
links.description: Providing technical documentation for eBPF.

links.title: BPF Documentation
links.description: Index for BPF-related documentation coming with the Linux kernel.

links.title: linux/Documentation/networking/filter.rst
links.description: eBPF specification (somewhat outdated; information should still be valid, but not exhaustive).

links.title: BPF Design Q&A
links.description: Frequently Asked Questions on the decisions behind the BPF infrastructure.

links.title: HOWTO interact with BPF subsystem
links.description: Frequently Asked Questions about contributing to eBPF development.

links.title: `bpf(2)`
links.description: Manual page about the `bpf()` system call, used to manage BPF programs and maps from userspace.

links.title: `tc-bpf(8)`
links.description: Manual page about using BPF with tc, including example commands and samples of code.

links.title: `bpf-helpers(7)` man page
links.description: Description of the in-kernel helper functions forming the BPF standard library.

links.title: RFC 9669 BPF Instruction Set Architecture
links.description: IETF specification for eBPF

links.title: Jesper Dangaard Brouer's documentation
links.description: Work in progress, contributions welcome.

links.title: List of BPF features per kernel version
A brief introduction to XDP and eBPF
links.description: An accessible introduction providing context, history, and details about the functioning of eBPF.

links.title: Ferris Ellis's blog posts about eBPF
links.description: They have a few posts about eBPF:

links.title: A BPF reference guide
links.description: About BPF C and bcc Python helpers, from bcc repository.

links.title: The BSD Packet Filter
links.description: An introduction mostly covering the tracing aspects.

links.title: BPF: tracing and more
links.description: An introduction mostly covering the tracing aspects.

links.title: Linux BPF Superpowers
links.description: An introduction mostly covering the tracing aspects, first part with flame graphs.

links.title: IO Visor
links.description: Also introduces [IO Visor project](https://www.iovisor.org/).

links.title: BPF -- in-kernel virtual machine
links.description: Presentation by the author of eBPF.

links.title: Extending extended BPF
links.description: A blog post from 2014 on the development of BPF and demonstrating what can be done with it, using an example of stateful socket filtering by attaching an eBPF program to a socket.

links.title: A Tour of Program Types
links.description: A description of all existing hooks for BPF program types, and of their interest.

links.title: BPF helper functions
links.description: A review of the kernel functions that can be called from within eBPF programs.

links.title: Communicating with Userspace
links.description: How BPF communicates with userspace - BPF maps, perf events, bpf_trace_printk.

links.title: Building BPF Programs
links.description: Setting up your environment to build BPF programs.

links.title: The BPF Bytecode and the BPF Verifier
links.description: How does BPF ensure that programs are safe?

links.title: Using BPF to do Packet Transformation
links.description: One eBPF usage about packet transformation.

links.title: Linux Kernel Observability through eBPF
links.description: A blog post covering the basics of eBPF as well as code samples in Go on how to build and load a minimal eBPF program into the kernel.

links.title: eBPF - From a Programmer's Perspective
links.description: A short paper describing the fundamentals of eBPF and how to get started with writing eBPF programs.

links.title: Cloudflare's blog posts on eBPF
links.description: Different blog posts about networking use cases and low-level aspects of eBPF.

links.title: Linux Extended BPF (eBPF) Tracing Tools
links.description: An in-depth collection of information around examples of performance analysis tools using eBPF. Contains also a section at the end of the page about other resources.

links.title: Beginner's guide to eBPF
links.description: A set of live-coding talks and the accompanying code examples, introducing eBPF programming using a variety of libraries and program types.

links.title: ebpf.io blog
links.description: Links to many community blogs posts.

links.title: eBPF and XDP walkthrough and recent (2017) updates
Advanced programmability and recent updates with tc's cls_bpf
links.description: Details on eBPF, its use for tunneling and encapsulation, direct packet access, and more.

links.title: cls_bpf/eBPF updates since netdev 1.1
links.description: Part of [this tc workshop](http://netdevconf.org/1.2/session.html?jamal-tc-workshop).

links.title: On getting tc classifier fully programmable with cls_bpf
links.description: Introduction to eBPF, including several features (map management, tail calls, verifier). The full paper [is also available here](http://www.netdevconf.org/1.1/proceedings/papers/On-getting-tc-classifier-fully-programmable-with-cls-bpf.pdf).

links.title: Linux Networking Explained
links.description: Linux networking internals, with a part about eBPF.

links.title: Full-system dynamic tracing on Linux using eBPF and bpftrace
links.description: A detailed introduction to tracing with eBPF, from listing the available trace points to running bpftrace programs.

links.title: Meet-cute between eBPF and Kernel Tracing
links.description: Kprobes, uprobes, ftrace.

links.title: Linux Kernel Tracing
links.description: Systemtap, Kernelshark, trace-cmd, LTTng, perf-tool, ftrace, hist-trigger, perf, function tracer, tracepoint, kprobe/uprobe, and more.

links.title: The eXpress Data Path
links.description: A very accessible introduction to XDP, providing sample code to show how to process packets.

links.title: eXpress Data Path (XDP)
links.description: The first presentation about XDP.

links.title: eXpress Data Path
links.description: Contains some benchmark results obtained with the mlx4 driver.

links.title: XDP − eXpress Data Path, Intro and future use-cases
links.description: Linux Kernel's fight against DPDK. Future plans (as of this writing) for XDP and comparison with DPDK.

links.title: XDP – eXpress Data Path, Used for DDoS protection
links.description: Details and use cases about XDP, with benchmark results, and code snippets for benchmarking as well as for basic DDoS protection with eBPF/XDP (based on an IP blacklisting scheme).

links.title: Memory vs. Networking, Provoking and fixing memory bottlenecks
links.description: Advanced details about current memory issues faced by XDP developers.

links.title: XDP for the Rest of Us
links.description: How to get started with eBPF and XDP for normal humans. Also summarized by Julia Evans on [her blog](http://jvns.ca/blog/2017/04/07/xdp-bpf-tutorial/).

links.title: XDP now with REDIRECT
links.description: Update on XDP, and in particular on the redirect actions.

links.title: High Speed Packet Filtering on Linux
links.description: About packet filtering on Linux, DDoS protection, packet processing in the kernel, kernel bypass, XDP and eBPF.

links.title: How to drop 10 million packets per second
links.description: Cloudflare's blog post talking about their move to using XDP for packet filtering.

links.title: AF_XDP
links.description: Kernel documentation on the AF_XDP address family.

links.title: Fast Packet Processing in Linux with AF_XDP
Why is the kernel community replacing iptables with BPF?
links.description: A blog post by Cilium on the motivations behind eBPF and bpfilter, with a couple examples and links to other projects using eBPF and bpfilter.

links.title: bpfilter: Linux firewall with eBPF sauce
links.description: Slides from a talk by Quentin Monnet with a background on eBPF and comparing bpfilter to iptables.

links.title: BPF Type Format (BTF)
links.description: Kernel documentation about BTF, explaining how to use it.

links.title: Enhancing the Linux kernel with BTF type information
links.description: A description of the work done with BTF to provide debugging information for BPF programs.

links.title: What is BTF (BPF Type Format)
links.description: A community-authored newsletter enriched with useful code illustrations and hands-on examples.

links.title: The BSD Packet Filter: A New Architecture for User-level Packet Capture
links.description: The original paper about (classic) BPF.

links.title: The FreeBSD manual page about BPF
Linux' packet mmap(2), BPF, and Netsniff-NG
tc and cls bpf: lightweight packet classifying with BPF
Introducing Cloudflare's BPF Tools
links.description: Usage of BPF bytecode with the `xt_bpf` module for iptables.

links.title: Comprehensive XDP offload---Handling the edge cases
links.description: An update on the topic above.

links.title: hBPF - eBPF in hardware
links.description: An eBPF CPU written for FPGAs.

links.title: OpenCSD eBPF SSD offloading
links.description: Computational Storage simulation (QEMU) platform with FUSE LFS filesystem for Zoned Namespaces NVMe SSDs using uBPF for compute kernel offloading, all in userspace.

links.title: Delilah: eBPF-offload on Computational Storage
links.description: Delilah is a Computational Storage Processor (CSP) built for eBPF offload to storage devices.

links.title: eBPF Party
links.description: Browser-based playground to learn, write, compile, and run eBPF programs.

links.title: bcc Python Developer Tutorial
links.description: Comes with bcc, but targets the Python bits across seventeen "lessons".

links.title: Building BPF applications with libbpf-bootstrap
links.description: Helps generate minimal or advanced templates to bootstrap your own applications (kernel side and user space management for maps and programs) with features like CO-RE, global variables, and ring buffer.

links.title: How I ended up writing opensnoop in pure C using eBPF
links.description: A thorough walk-through of how to write eBPF programs, first using only bpf() syscall, and then libbpf library, with reproducible code examples.

links.title: Linux Tracing Workshops Materials
links.description: Involves the use of several BPF tools for tracing.

links.title: Tracing a packet journey using Linux tracepoints, perf and eBPF
links.description: Troubleshooting ping requests and replies with perf and bcc programs.

links.title: Open NFP platform
links.description: Operated by Netronome: some tutorials for network-related eBPF use cases, including an eBPF Offload Starting Guide.

links.title: XDP Hands-On Tutorial
links.description: A progressive (three levels of difficulty) tutorial to learn how to process packets with XDP.

links.title: All your tracing are belong to BPF
links.description: A step-by-step walkthrough to integrate tracing capabilities in your C++ applications with the LLVM libraries.

links.title: Firewalling with BPF/XDP: Examples and Deep Dive
links.description: A simple guide to build basic firewalls with TC and XDP.

links.title: A Deep Dive into eBPF: Writing an Efficient DNS Monitoring.
links.description: A detailed explanation of methods used to capture DNS requests at the socket filter layer.

links.title: eBPF Developer Tutorial - Learn eBPF by examples
links.description: Start with eBPF basics and progress to advanced topics using 20+ hands-on tutorials and examples. Covers performance, networking, and security with libbpf and CO-RE. Available in Chinese and English.

links.title: Catch Performance Regressions in eBPF
links.description: A step-by-step guide to benchmarking both the client and kernel eBPF code written in Rust.

links.title: Loops and Iterators in eBPF
links.description: Newsletter about all the ways to loop and iterate in eBPF.

links.title: What Insights Can eBPF Provide into Real-Time SSL/TLS Encrypted Traffic and How?
links.description: A step-by-step guide how eBPF can observe encrypted network traffic.

links.title: Can eBPF Detect Redis Message Patterns Before They Become Problems?
links.description: A step-by-step guide how eBPF can observe Redis communication between client and server.

links.title: Transparent Proxy Implementation using eBPF and Go
links.description: A step-by-step guide on how to implement a transparent proxy using eBPF.

links.title: eBPF-Powered Load Balancing
links.description: Learn how eBPF can infer custom load-balancing for services listening on the same port, through the SO_REUSEPORT TCP option.

links.title: Unit Testing eBPF Programs
links.description: Learn how you can unit test your eBPF programs using libbpf.

links.title: Accelerating Local Socket Communication using eBPF
links.description: Learn how eBPF can speed-up local socket communication up to 30%.

links.title: Writing a basic continuous profiler
links.description: A step-by-step guide to write an appliation continuous profiler leveraging the eBPF instrumentation, with a complete project as a reference.

links.title: Inspektor Gadget - Hello world gadget
links.description: An introductory guide to writing image-based eBPF gadgets and sharing them via OCI registries.

links.title: Inspektor Gadget - Hello world gadget with Wasm
links.description: An introductory guide to writing image-based eBPF gadgets and performing post-processing with WASM.

links.title: ebpf.io labs
links.description: List of community developed labs.

links.title: linux/samples/bpf/
links.description: In the kernel tree: some sample eBPF programs.

links.title: linux/tools/testing/selftests/bpf
links.description: In the kernel tree: Linux BPF selftests, with many eBPF programs.

links.title: prototype-kernel/kernel/samples/bpf
links.description: Jesper Dangaard Brouer's prototype-kernel repository contains some additional examples that can be compiled outside of kernel infrastructure.

links.title: iproute2/examples/bpf/
links.description: Some networking programs to attach to the TC interface.

links.title: Netronome sample network applications
links.description: Provides basic but complete examples of eBPF applications also compatible with hardware offload.

links.title: bcc/examples
links.description: Examples coming along with the bcc tools, mostly about tracing.

links.title: bcc/tools
links.description: These tools themselves can be seen as example use cases for BPF programs, mostly for tracing and monitoring. bcc tools have been packaged for some Linux distributions.

links.title: MPLSinIP sample
links.description: A heavily commented sample demonstrating how to encapsulate and decapsulate MPLS within IP. The code is commented for those new to BPF development.

links.title: ebpf-samples
links.description: A collection of compiled (as ELF object files) samples gathered from several projects, primarily intended to serve as test cases for user space verifiers.

links.title: ebpf-kill-example
links.description: A fully documented and tested example of an eBPF probe that logs all force-kills and prints them out in user-space.

links.title: redbpf examples
links.description: Example programs for using RedBPF to write eBPF programs in Rust.

links.title: XDP/TC-eBPF example
links.description: Program that uses XDP/TC-eBPF to provide statefull firewalling and socket redirection.

links.title: bcc
links.description: Framework and set of tools - One way to handle BPF programs, in particular for tracing and monitoring. Also includes some utilities that may help inspect maps or programs on the system.

links.title: Lua front-end for BCC
links.description: Another alternative to C, and even to most of the Python code used in bcc.

links.title: iproute2
links.description: Package containing tools for network management on Linux. In particular, it contains `tc`, used to manage eBPF filters and actions, and `ip`, used to manage XDP programs. Most of the code related to BPF is in lib/bpf.c.

links.title: iproute2-next
links.description: The development tree, synchronised with net-next.

links.title: libbpf
links.description: A C library used for handling BPF objects (programs and maps), and manipulating ELF object files containing them. It is shipped with the kernel and [mirrored on GitHub](https://github.com/libbpf/libbpf).

links.title: libbpf-bootstrap
links.description: Scaffolding for BPF application development with libbpf and BPF CO-RE.

links.title: libbpfgo
links.description: eBPF library for Go, powered by libbpf.

links.title: aya-template
links.description: Templates for writing BPF applications in Aya that can be used with [`cargo generate`](https://github.com/cargo-generate/cargo-generate).

links.title: zbpf
links.description: A pure Zig framework for writing cross platform eBPF programs, powered by libbpf and Zig toolchain.

links.title: eunomia-bpf
links.description: A compilation framework and runtime library to build, distribute, dynamically load, and run CO-RE eBPF applications in multiple languages and WebAssembly. It supports writing eBPF kernel code only (to build simple CO-RE libbpf eBPF applications), writing the kernel part in both BCC and libbpf styles, and writing userspace in multiple languages in a WASM module and distributing it with simple JSON data or WASM OCI images. The runtime is based on libbpf only and provides CO-RE to BCC-style eBPF programs without depending on the LLVM library.

links.title: bpftool
links.description: Also some other tools in the kernel tree, under [linux/tools/net/](https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/tree/tools/net?h=v4.14) for versions earlier than 4.15, or [linux/tools/bpf/](https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/davem/net-next.git/tree/tools/bpf) after that:
links.category: bpftool and Other Tools from the Kernel Tree

links.title: `bpf_asm`
links.description: A minimal cBPF assembler.
links.category: bpftool and Other Tools from the Kernel Tree

links.title: `bpf_dbg`
links.description: A small debugger for cBPF programs.
links.category: bpftool and Other Tools from the Kernel Tree

links.title: `bpf_jit_disasm`
links.description: A disassembler for both BPF flavors and could be highly useful for JIT debugging.
links.category: bpftool and Other Tools from the Kernel Tree

links.title: uBPF
links.description: Written in C. Contains an interpreter, a JIT compiler for x86_64 architecture, an assembler and a disassembler.

links.title: A generic implementation
links.description: With support for FreeBSD kernel, FreeBSD user space, Linux kernel, Linux user space and macOS user space. Used for the [VALE software switch](https://www.unix.com/man-page/freebsd/4/vale/)'s [BPF extension module](https://github.com/YutaroHayakawa/vale-bpf).

links.title: rbpf
links.description: Written in Rust. Interpreter for Linux, macOS and Windows, and JIT-compiler for x86_64 under Linux.

links.title: PREVAIL
links.description: A user space verifier for eBPF [using an abstract interpretation layer](https://elazarg.github.io/pldi19main-final.pdf), with support for loops.

links.title: wachy
links.description: A tracing profiler that aims to make eBPF uprobe-based debugging easier to use. This is done by displaying traces in a UI next to the source code and allowing interactive drilldown analysis.

links.title: eBPF for Windows
links.description: This project is a work-in-progress that allows using existing eBPF toolchains and APIs familiar in the Linux ecosystem to be used on top of Windows.

links.title: bpfcompat
links.description: Boots real Linux kernels in disposable QEMU/KVM VMs to load- and attach-validate compiled eBPF objects across a multi-distro, multi-architecture kernel matrix, classifying failures (missing BTF, unsupported map/program type, CO-RE relocations); runs as a CI gate and GitHub Action.

links.title: OvS Orbit episode (#11), called P4 on the Edge
links.description: Related to the former item. Audio interview of John Fastabend by Ben Pfaff, one of the core maintainers of Open vSwitch.

links.title: Cilium: Networking & Security for Containers with BPF & XDP
links.description: Also featuring a load balancer use case

links.title: Cilium: Networking & Security for Containers with BPF & XDP
links.description: [video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TnJF7ht3ZYc&list=PLkA60AVN3hh8oPas3cq2VA9xB7WazcIgs)

links.title: Cilium: Fast IPv6 container Networking with BPF and XDP
OvS Orbit episode (#4)
links.description: Interview of Thomas Graf by Ben Pfaff.

links.title: A podcast interviewing Thomas Graf
links.description: Ivan Pepelnjak interviewing Thomas, October 2016, on eBPF, P4, XDP and Cilium.

links.title: Katran
links.description: A layer 4 load-balancer based on XDP, open-sourced by Facebook.

links.title: DPDK has a poll-mode driver (PMD) based on AF_XDP
CETH for XDP
links.description: Common Ethernet Driver Framework for faster network I/O, a technology initiated by Mellanox.

links.title: "eBPF and XDP" section of Suricata documentation
SEPTun-Mark-II
links.description: Extreme Performance Tuning guide - Mark II.

links.title: A blog post introducing the "capture bypass" feature
The adventures of a Suricate in eBPF land
eBPF and XDP seen from the eyes of a meerkat
Project Calico
links.description: Calico is an open source networking and network security solution for containers, virtual machines, and native host-based workloads. Calico's eBPF data plane delivers a low latency, high throughput data plane with a rich network security policy model.

links.title: Enabling eBPF data plane with Calico
merbridge
links.description: Use eBPF to speed up your Service Mesh. Merbridge replaces iptables rules with eBPF to intercept traffic. It also combines msg_redirect to reduce latency with a shortened datapath between sidecars and services.

links.title: PcapPlusPlus
links.description: An open-source C++ library for capturing, parsing and crafting network packets. It features a C++ interface for creating AF_XDP sockets, making it easy to [send and receive packets through them](https://pcapplusplus.github.io/docs/next/features#af_xdp-support-beta).

links.title: ApFree WiFiDog
links.description: A high performance and lightweight captive portal solution for wireless networks. It leverages eBPF for traffic control and deep packet inspection capabilities, with plans to gradually replace nftables firewall functionality with eBPF-based solutions.

links.title: ipx_wrap
links.description: A proof-of-concept IPX implementation for Linux using eBPF.

links.title: InKeV: In-Kernel Distributed Network Virtualization for DCN
DEEP-mon
links.description: Helps with measuring power consumption for servers and uses eBPF programs for in-kernel aggregation of data.

links.title: pixie
links.description: Observability for Kubernetes using eBPF. Features include protocol tracing, application profiling, and support for distributed bpftrace deployments.

links.title: SkyWalking Rover
links.description: [Apache SkyWalking](https://skywalking.apache.org/) is an open-source Application Performance Monitoring (APM) platform specially designed for distributed systems with microservices, cloud-native and container-based (Kubernetes) architectures. SkyWalking Rover is an eBPF-based profiler and metrics collector for C, C++, Golang, and Rust applications.

links.title: parca-agent
links.description: eBPF based always-on continuous profiler for analysis of CPU and memory usage, down to the line number and throughout time.

links.title: rbperf
links.description: Sampling profiler and tracer for Ruby.

links.title: rstat
links.description: Sub-millisecond system monitoring using eBPF tracepoints on sched_switch, sched_process_exit, and sched_process_free, with zero heap allocations in steady state.

links.title: Hubble
links.description: Network, service and security observability for Kubernetes using eBPF.

links.title: Ingero
links.description: eBPF-based GPU causal observability agent. Traces CUDA Runtime and Driver APIs via uprobes and host kernel events via tracepoints to build causal chains explaining GPU latency, with <2% overhead.

links.title: Caretta
links.description: Instant Kubernetes service dependency map generated by eBPF, right to a Grafana instance.

links.title: kpod-metrics
links.description: eBPF-based pod-level kernel metrics collector for Kubernetes. Exports per-pod CPU, network, memory, syscall, disk I/O, and L7 protocol metrics to Prometheus. BPF programs are defined using a [Kotlin DSL](https://github.com/pjs7678/kotlin-ebpf-dsl) instead of C.

links.title: DeepFlow
links.description: Instant observability for cloud-native and AI applications based on eBPF.

links.title: Coroot
links.description: Coroot is an open-source APM and observability tool, a DataDog and NewRelic alternative.

links.title: kyanos
links.description: Kyanos is an eBPF-based network issue analysis tool that enables you to capture network requests, such as HTTP, Redis, and MySQL requests.

links.title: eTraceGen
links.description: eTraceGen is a Linux telemetry engine built with eBPF and Modern C++ that captures kernel-level events for processes, files, system calls, and network with a modular pipeline for decoding, enrichment, filtering, and JSON output.

links.title: Falco
links.description: A cloud-native runtime security project used as a Kubernetes threat detection engine.

links.title: Sysmon for Linux
links.description: A security monitoring tool. It depends on [SysinternalsEBPF](https://github.com/Sysinternals/SysinternalsEBPF).

links.title: Red Canary Linux Agent
links.description: Red Canary has started to incorporate eBPF to their Linux security sensor.

links.title: Tracee
links.description: A runtime security and forensics tool for Linux which uses eBPF technology to trace the system and applications at runtime, and analyze collected events to detect suspicious behavioral patterns.

links.title: redcanary-ebpf-sensor
links.description: A set of BPF programs that gather security relevant event data from the Linux kernel. The BPF programs are combined into a single ELF file from which individual probes can be selectively loaded, depending on the running operating system and kernel version.

links.title: bpflock - Lock Linux machines
links.description: An eBPF driven security tool for locking and auditing Linux machines.

links.title: Tetragon
links.description: Kubernetes-aware, eBPF-based security observability and runtime enforcement.

links.title: harpoon
links.description: Trace syscalls from user-space functions, by using eBPF.

links.title: Synapse
links.description: Extended detection and response (XDR) with eBPF-powered firewall and proxy, to protect your Linux servers.

links.title: BPFJailer
links.description: BpfJailer is an eBPF-based process jailing system that provides mandatory access control (MAC) for Linux.

links.title: Bombini
links.description: An eBPF-based security agent written entirely in Rust using the [Aya](https://github.com/aya-rs/aya) library and built on LSM (Linux Security Module) BPF hooks.

links.title: owLSM
links.description: Open source agent that implements a stateful Sigma rules engine focused on monitoring and prevention using eBPF LSM.

links.title: scx
links.description: sched_ext schedulers and tools.

links.title: Gthulhu
links.description: Gthulhu optimizes cloud-native workloads using the Linux Scheduler Extension for different application scenarios.

links.title: ply
links.description: A small but flexible open source dynamic tracer for Linux, with features similar to the bcc tools, but with a simpler language inspired by awk and DTrace.

links.title: bpftrace
links.description: A tool for tracing with its own high-level tracing language. It is flexible enough to be envisioned as a Linux replacement for DTrace and SystemTap.

links.title: bpftrace Cheat Sheet
links.description: Summary and cheat sheet for programming in bpftrace. Contains information about syntax, probe types, variables and functions.

links.title: kubectl trace
links.description: A kubectl plug-in for executing bpftrace programs in a Kubernetes cluster.

links.title: inspektor-gadget
links.description: A collection tools and framework for data collection and system inspection on Kubernetes clusters and Linux hosts using eBPF.

links.title: bpfd
links.description: Framework for running BPF programs with rules on Linux as a daemon. Container aware.

links.title: BPFd
links.description: A distinct BPF daemon, trying to leverage the flexibility of the bcc tools to trace and debug remote targets, and in particular devices running with Android.

links.title: adeb
links.description: A Linux shell environment for using tracing tools on Android with BPFd.

links.title: greggd
links.description: System daemon to compile and load eBPF programs into the kernel, and forward program output to socket for metric aggregation.

links.title: FUSE
links.description: Considers using eBPF.

links.title: upf-bpf
links.description: An in-kernel solution based on XDP for 5G UPF.

links.title: redbpf
links.description: Tooling and framework to write eBPF code in Rust efficiently.

links.title: ebpf-explorer
links.description: A web interface to explore system's maps and programs.

links.title: ebpfmon
links.description: A TUI (terminal user interface) application for real time monitoring of eBPF programs.

links.title: bpfman
links.description: An eBPF Manager for Linux and Kubernetes. Includes a built-in program loader that supports program cooperation for XDP and TC programs, as well as deployment of eBPF programs from OCI images.

links.title: ptcpdump
links.description: A process-aware, eBPF-based tcpdump-like tool.

links.title: oryx
links.description: A TUI for sniffing network traffic using eBPF on Linux.

links.title: GhostScope
links.description: A DWARF-aware eBPF tracer for source-level userspace tracing, with an interactive TUI and a scriptable CLI.

links.title: AgentSight
links.description: Zero-instrumentation eBPF observability for LLM and coding agents, capturing syscall-level traces (file, network, process) without modifying the agent.

links.title: ActPlane
links.description: OS-level agent harness that compiles a policy DSL to an in-kernel eBPF engine for labeled information-flow control at the syscall boundary, enforcing constraints across any tool or subprocess.

links.title: Embrace The Red: Offensive BPF!
links.description: A series of posts around the introduction into BPF with a focus to an offensive setting, and also how its misuse can be detected. Posts include discussions on the rootkit capabilities of eBPF, or on which tracing type is needed for different use cases.

links.title: eBPF: Block Linux Fileless Payload "Malware" Execution with BPF LSM
links.description: Blog post about how BPF can help detection and blocking fileless malware.

links.title: Blackhat 2021: With Friends Like eBPF, Who Needs Enemies?
links.description: Talk about an eBPF rootkit and how the capabilities of eBPF could be abused. The rootkit was also the object of a talk at Defcon, [eBPF, I thought we were friends !](https://defcon.org/html/defcon-29/dc-29-speakers.html#fournier).

links.title: ebpfkit
links.description: A rootkit that leverages multiple eBPF features to implement offensive security techniques.

links.title: ebpfkit-monitor
links.description: An utility to statically analyze eBPF bytecode or monitor suspicious eBPF activity at runtime. It was specifically designed to detect ebpfkit.

links.title: Bad BPF
links.description: A collection of malicious eBPF programs that make use of eBPF's ability to read and write user data in between the usermode program and the kernel.

links.title: TripleCross
links.description: A Linux eBPF rootkit with a backdoor, C2, library injection, execution hijacking, persistence and stealth capabilities.

links.title: linux/include/linux/bpf.h
links.description: with [linux/include/uapi/bpf.h](https://git.kernel.org/cgit/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/tree/include/uapi/linux/bpf.h): definitions related to eBPF, to be used respectively in the kernel and to interface with userspace programs.

links.title: linux/include/linux/filter.h
links.description: with [linux/include/uapi/filter.h](https://git.kernel.org/cgit/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/tree/include/uapi/linux/filter.h): information used to run the BPF programs themselves.

links.title: linux/kernel/bpf/
links.description: This directory contains most of BPF-related code. In particular, those files are worth of interest:

links.title: `syscall.c`
links.description: Different operations permitted by the system call, such as program loading or map management.

links.title: `core.c`
links.description: BPF interpreter.

links.title: `verifier.c`
links.description: BPF verifier.

links.title: linux/net/core/filter.c
links.description: Functions and eBPF helpers related to networking (TC, XDP etc.); also contains the code to migrate cBPF bytecode to eBPF (all cBPF programs are translated to eBPF in recent kernels).

links.title: linux/kernel/trace/bpf_trace.c
links.description: Functions and eBPF helpers related to tracing and monitoring (kprobes, tracepoints, etc.).

links.title: linux/net/sched/
links.description: and in particular in files `act_bpf.c` (action) and `cls_bpf.c` (filter): code related to BPF actions and filters with TC.

links.title: linux/net/core/dev.c
links.description: contains the function `dev_change_xdp_fd()` that is called through a Netlink command to hook a XDP program to a device, after is has been loaded into the kernel from user space. This function in turns uses a callback from the relevant driver.

links.title: The bpf-next tree
links.description: BPF patches land in this tree. It is regularly merged into [net-next](https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/davem/net-next.git), which is itself merged for each release to Linus' tree.

links.title: Kernel documentation
links.description: About contributions to BPF.

links.title: The netdev mailing list
links.description: Mailing list for Linux kernel networking stack development. All patches are sent there for review and inclusion.

links.title: XDP-newbies
links.description: A mailing list specially dedicated to XDP programming (both for architecture or for asking for help).

links.title: The XDP Collaboration Project
links.description: A GitHub repository with notes and ideas regarding the future evolutions of XDP.
